title: Americans hate AI so much that politicians are starting to lose their jobs over it | Fortune
excerpt: Only 26% of Americans view AI positively, and Utah's ouster of Senate leader Stuart Adams over a data center deal suggests the anger is now costing jobs.
content_html:
Data center projects continue to generate controversy around the country. In part, that’s because a variety of different groups have competing interests – some in favor of them, some opposed and others with no direct view on data centers themselves, but with concerns that relate to aspects of data center operations and effects.

As a scholar of environmental justice and urban land use, I’ve seen these various conflicting forces at work in Michigan. More than 30 large and small data center projects have been proposed in the state in the past two years alone, including one by the university where I work.

Gov. Gretchen Whitmer is enthusiastic about bringing technology companies to the state, even posing with tech company CEOs in photo ops at the sites of proposed data centers.

But not everyone is as excited. In just one example of the opposition these projects can face, the local water company where I live, the Ypsilanti Community Utilities Authority, told the state it would not supply water for cooling a data center that the University of Michigan and Los Alamos National Laboratory had proposed within its service area.

So the University of Michigan proposed a different site in the next town over, Superior Township. That town manages its own water but gets its supply by buying it from both the Ypsilanti Community Utilities Authority and Ann Arbor township.

A look at some of the forces at play around these projects reveals the deep issues they raise. The fights about data centers can often take the form of collisions between companies and community members. But they also reflect conflict about social values, democratic systems and capitalist interests.

Tech companies

Tech companies have an obvious desire to store and process ever more data. And many key aspects of society rely on the data centers that have been built over the decades, serving websites, handling online purchases and delivering emails, text messages and alert notices.

The latest push is related to the anticipated demand for artificial intelligence systems in the business world and in people’s lives – and the resulting increased demand for computing capacity to process all those requests and filter through all the available information to provide responses.

The corporate pressures to grow, to continue increasing profits for shareholders and to squeeze more revenue from workers and assets keep these tech companies seeking more land on which to build more data centers. And the Michigan Legislature has openly courted tech companies, passing laws that exempt data center operators from sales and use taxes they would otherwise have to pay.

Electricity companies

Many power companies are regulated by state or local governments and are not allowed to make profits off maintaining existing service or selling electricity. Their profits come from building new power plants, new transformers and substations, and new power lines – and passing those costs along to customers, with a markup for profit.

A data center can require significant investment in power generation and transmission – a data center proposed by Google in Van Buren Township, Michigan, for example, would require “2.7 gigawatts of electricity – a massive amount of power equivalent to the demand of about 2 million homes,” according to a local news report.

So a power company is likely to be eager to capitalize on the opportunity for a new major customer and may be willing to endure some amount of public backlash.

Power companies that operate in small geographic areas are highly dependent on the success of the municipalities from which they draw their customers and in which they build their infrastructure. In these situations, they have a vested interest in local economic growth, specifically the movement to the area of companies that supply jobs and, therefore, workers. This drives them to get involved in local political decision-making by lobbying for zoning changes to enable data centers.

Larger utility companies that operate regionally or across entire states are less tied to the economic success of a specific municipality within their service area. But they are also able to target their influence at state lawmakers. For example, DTE Energy assured state lawmakers that the proposed data center in Van Buren Township would not raise customers’ power rates.

Community leaders

Community leaders and elected officials are often interested in the jobs that tech companies promise will come with the data centers, so they tend to support approval of permits, zoning amendments or other legal changes.

Although data centers don’t really create very many jobs, they do have the potential to contribute to local property taxes, which can appeal to small towns without many other options.

Cities and towns are always on the hunt for growth opportunities, seeking additional tax revenue. That can lead them to feel pressure to build new roads, modify their land-use ordinances and approve requests from companies wishing to build within their boundaries.

My research has found that this pressure exists even in shrinking or small cities. This pressure has increased as cities have sought to reduce taxes on residents. The solution they see to getting fewer dollars out of existing residents is to bring in more businesses, more industry and more new residents. That makes them interested in welcoming data centers.

However, not every community leader is on board with data centers. Some oppose them but feel hamstrung or powerless to stop or slow their progress, as occurred in Ypsilanti Township and in nearby Saline Township as well.

Everyday people

In many communities, everyday people concerned with noise, land use, water use and power use oppose the data centers.

They are also concerned about the costs data centers may impose on utility ratepayers, taxpayers and those who must experience the environmental effects. People’s power, exercised through democratic processes such as public hearings, ordinance revisions and elections, can be overwhelmed by the tech companies and aligned groups.

But voters do have power. In June 2026, Utah voters unseated a longtime legislative leader, state Senate President Stuart Adams, who had helped get approval for a massive data center in the northwestern part of the state.

Whether a data center moves forward in a municipality ends up being a matter of how public officials sort through the motivations and sociopolitical power of all these players.

Lauren Mullenbach, Assistant Research Scientist in Environmental Justice, University of Michigan

This article is republished from The Conversation under a Creative Commons license. Read the original article.

Some oppose them but feel hamstrung or powerless to stop or slow their progress, as occurred in Ypsilanti Township and in nearby Saline Township as well. People in Saline, Mich., hold signs opposing a data center proposed in their community. blocks.html_en: Data center projects continue to generate controversy around the country. In part, that’s because a variety of different groups have competing interests – some in favor of them, some opposed and others with no direct view on data centers themselves, but with concerns that relate to aspects of data center operations and effects.

sentences.en: Data center projects continue to generate controversy around the country.
sentences.zh: 数据中心项目继续在全国范围内引发争议。

sentences.en: In part, that’s because a variety of different groups have competing interests – some in favor of them, some opposed and others with no direct view on data centers themselves, but with concerns that relate to aspects of data center operations and effects.
sentences.zh: 在某种程度上，这是因为各种不同的群体有着相互竞争的利益——一些人支持他们，一些人反对，另一些人对数据中心本身没有直接看法，但对数据中心运营和影响的各个方面表示担忧。

blocks.html_en: As a scholar of environmental justice and urban land use, I’ve seen these various conflicting forces at work in Michigan. More than 30 large and small data center projects have been proposed in the state in the past two years alone, including one by the university where I work.

sentences.en: As a scholar of environmental justice and urban land use, I’ve seen these various conflicting forces at work in Michigan.
sentences.zh: 作为环境正义和城市土地利用的学者，我在密歇根州看到了各种相互冲突的力量在发挥作用。

sentences.en: More than 30 large and small data center projects have been proposed in the state in the past two years alone, including one by the university where I work.
sentences.zh: 仅过去两年，该州就提出了 30 多个大大小小的数据中心项目，其中包括我工作的大学的一个。

sentences.en: Michigan Gov.
sentences.zh: 密歇根州州长

sentences.en: Gretchen Whitmer, center, has been criticized for participating in this photo op at the construction site for a new data center in her state.
sentences.zh: 格雷琴·惠特默（中）因参与该州新数据中心建筑工地的这张照片而受到批评。

sentences.en: Related Digital via Planet Detroit
sentences.zh: 相关数字来自底特律星球

blocks.html_en: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer is enthusiastic about bringing technology companies to the state, even posing with tech company CEOs in photo ops at the sites of proposed data centers.

sentences.en: Gov.
sentences.zh: 政府。

sentences.en: Gretchen Whitmer is enthusiastic about bringing technology companies to the state, even posing with tech company CEOs in photo ops at the sites of proposed data centers.
sentences.zh: 格雷琴·惠特默 (Gretchen Whitmer) 热衷于将科技公司引入该州，甚至在拟议的数据中心所在地与科技公司首席执行官合影留念。

blocks.html_en: But not everyone is as excited. In just one example of the opposition these projects can face, the local water company where I live, the Ypsilanti Community Utilities Authority, told the state it would not supply water for cooling a data center that the University of Michigan and Los Alamos National Laboratory had proposed within its service area.

sentences.en: But not everyone is as excited.
sentences.zh: 但并不是每个人都那么兴奋。

sentences.en: In just one example of the opposition these projects can face, the local water company where I live, the Ypsilanti Community Utilities Authority, told the state it would not supply water for cooling a data center that the University of Michigan and Los Alamos National Laboratory had proposed within its service area.
sentences.zh: 这些项目可能面临的反对只是一个例子，我居住的当地自来水公司伊普西兰蒂社区公用事业管理局告诉州政府，它不会为密歇根大学和洛斯阿拉莫斯国家实验室在其服务区域内提议的数据中心提供冷却水。

blocks.html_en: So the University of Michigan proposed a different site in the next town over, Superior Township. That town manages its own water but gets its supply by buying it from both the Ypsilanti Community Utilities Authority and Ann Arbor township.

sentences.en: So the University of Michigan proposed a different site in the next town over, Superior Township.
sentences.zh: 因此，密歇根大学提议在邻近的苏必利尔镇建立一个不同的地点。

sentences.en: That town manages its own water but gets its supply by buying it from both the Ypsilanti Community Utilities Authority and Ann Arbor township.
sentences.zh: 该镇自己管理水源，但通过从伊普西兰蒂社区公用事业管理局和安娜堡镇购买水来获得供水。

blocks.html_en: A look at some of the forces at play around these projects reveals the deep issues they raise. The fights about data centers can often take the form of collisions between companies and community members. But they also reflect conflict about social values, democratic systems and capitalist interests.

sentences.en: A look at some of the forces at play around these projects reveals the deep issues they raise.
sentences.zh: 看看围绕这些项目发挥作用的一些力量，就会发现它们提出的深层次问题。

sentences.en: The fights about data centers can often take the form of collisions between companies and community members.
sentences.zh: 关于数据中心的争论常常表现为公司和社区成员之间的冲突。

sentences.en: But they also reflect conflict about social values, democratic systems and capitalist interests.
sentences.zh: 但它们也反映了社会价值观、民主制度和资本主义利益的冲突。

blocks.html_en: Tech companies

sentences.en: Tech companies
sentences.zh: 科技公司

blocks.html_en: Tech companies have an obvious desire to store and process ever more data. And many key aspects of society rely on the data centers that have been built over the decades, serving websites, handling online purchases and delivering emails, text messages and alert notices.

sentences.en: Tech companies have an obvious desire to store and process ever more data.
sentences.zh: 科技公司显然希望存储和处理更多的数据。

sentences.en: And many key aspects of society rely on the data centers that have been built over the decades, serving websites, handling online purchases and delivering emails, text messages and alert notices.
sentences.zh: 社会的许多关键方面都依赖于几十年来建立的数据中心，为网站提供服务，处理在线购买以及发送电子邮件、短信和警报通知。

blocks.html_en: The latest push is related to the anticipated demand for artificial intelligence systems in the business world and in people’s lives – and the resulting increased demand for computing capacity to process all those requests and filter through all the available information to provide responses.

sentences.en: The latest push is related to the anticipated demand for artificial intelligence systems in the business world and in people’s lives – and the resulting increased demand for computing capacity to process all those requests and filter through all the available information to provide responses.
sentences.zh: 最新的推动与商业世界和人们生活中对人工智能系统的预期需求有关，以及由此产生的对计算能力的需求增加，以处理所有这些请求并过滤所有可用信息以提供响应。

blocks.html_en: The corporate pressures to grow, to continue increasing profits for shareholders and to squeeze more revenue from workers and assets keep these tech companies seeking more land on which to build more data centers. And the Michigan Legislature has openly courted tech companies, passing laws that exempt data center operators from sales and use taxes they would otherwise have to pay.

sentences.en: The corporate pressures to grow, to continue increasing profits for shareholders and to squeeze more revenue from workers and assets keep these tech companies seeking more land on which to build more data centers.
sentences.zh: 企业发展的压力、继续为股东增加利润以及从员工和资产中榨取更多收入的压力，促使这些科技公司寻求更多土地来建设更多数据中心。

sentences.en: And the Michigan Legislature has openly courted tech companies, passing laws that exempt data center operators from sales and use taxes they would otherwise have to pay.
sentences.zh: 密歇根州立法机关公开向科技公司示好，通过了法律，免除数据中心运营商原本必须缴纳的销售税和使用税。

sentences.en: Data centers, like this one in Georgia, use large amounts of electricity.
sentences.zh: 像佐治亚州的数据中心一样，数据中心使用大量电力。

sentences.en: AP Photo/Mike Stewart
sentences.zh: 美联社照片/迈克·斯图尔特

blocks.html_en: Electricity companies

sentences.zh: 电力公司

blocks.html_en: Many power companies are regulated by state or local governments and are not allowed to make profits off maintaining existing service or selling electricity. Their profits come from building new power plants, new transformers and substations, and new power lines – and passing those costs along to customers, with a markup for profit.

sentences.en: Their profits come from building new power plants, new transformers and substations, and new power lines – and passing those costs along to customers, with a markup for profit.
sentences.zh: 他们的利润来自建造新发电厂、新变压器和变电站以及新电力线，并将这些成本转嫁给客户，并提高利润。

blocks.html_en: A data center can require significant investment in power generation and transmission – a data center proposed by Google in Van Buren Township, Michigan, for example, would require “2.7 gigawatts of electricity – a massive amount of power equivalent to the demand of about 2 million homes,” according to a local news report.

sentences.zh: 据当地新闻报道，数据中心可能需要在发电和输电方面进行大量投资——例如，谷歌在密歇根州范布伦镇提议的数据中心将需要“2.7吉瓦的电力——相当于大约200万个家庭的电力需求”。

blocks.html_en: So a power company is likely to be eager to capitalize on the opportunity for a new major customer and may be willing to endure some amount of public backlash.

sentences.en: So a power company is likely to be eager to capitalize on the opportunity for a new major customer and may be willing to endure some amount of public backlash.

sentences.zh: 但他们也能够将影响力瞄准州立法者。

sentences.zh: 然而，并非每个社区领袖都支持数据中心。

sentences.en: Some oppose them but feel hamstrung or powerless to stop or slow their progress, as occurred in Ypsilanti Township and in nearby Saline Township as well.
sentences.zh: 有些人反对他们，但感到束手无策或无力​​阻止或减缓他们的进展，正如伊普西兰蒂镇和附近的萨林镇所发生的那样。

sentences.en: People in Saline, Mich., hold signs opposing a data center proposed in their community.
sentences.zh: 密歇根州萨林市的人们举着标语反对在他们的社区建立数据中心。

sentences.en: Jim West/UCG/Universal Images Group via Getty Images
sentences.zh: 吉姆·韦斯特/UCG/环球影像集团（盖蒂图片社）

blocks.html_en: Everyday people

sentences.en: Everyday people
sentences.zh: 日常人

blocks.html_en: In many communities, everyday people concerned with noise, land use, water use and power use oppose the data centers.

sentences.en: In many communities, everyday people concerned with noise, land use, water use and power use oppose the data centers.
sentences.zh: 在许多社区，关心噪音、土地使用、水和电力使用的普通人反对数据中心。

blocks.html_en: They are also concerned about the costs data centers may impose on utility ratepayers, taxpayers and those who must experience the environmental effects. People’s power, exercised through democratic processes such as public hearings, ordinance revisions and elections, can be overwhelmed by the tech companies and aligned groups.

sentences.en: They are also concerned about the costs data centers may impose on utility ratepayers, taxpayers and those who must experience the environmental effects.
sentences.zh: 他们还担心数据中心可能会给公用事业纳税人、纳税人和那些必须承受环境影响的人带来的成本。

sentences.en: People’s power, exercised through democratic processes such as public hearings, ordinance revisions and elections, can be overwhelmed by the tech companies and aligned groups.
sentences.zh: 通过公开听证会、条例修订和选举等民主程序行使的人民权力可能会被科技公司和结盟团体压垮。

blocks.html_en: But voters do have power. In June 2026, Utah voters unseated a longtime legislative leader, state Senate President Stuart Adams, who had helped get approval for a massive data center in the northwestern part of the state.

sentences.en: But voters do have power.
sentences.zh: 但选民确实有权力。

sentences.en: In June 2026, Utah voters unseated a longtime legislative leader, state Senate President Stuart Adams, who had helped get approval for a massive data center in the northwestern part of the state.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 6 月，犹他州选民罢免了长期立法领袖、州参议院主席斯图尔特·亚当斯 (Stuart Adams)，他曾帮助获得在该州西北部建立大型数据中心的批准。

blocks.html_en: Whether a data center moves forward in a municipality ends up being a matter of how public officials sort through the motivations and sociopolitical power of all these players.

sentences.en: Whether a data center moves forward in a municipality ends up being a matter of how public officials sort through the motivations and sociopolitical power of all these players.
sentences.zh: 数据中心能否在一个城市向前发展最终取决于政府官员如何理清所有这些参与者的动机和社会政治力量。

blocks.html_en: Lauren Mullenbach, Assistant Research Scientist in Environmental Justice, University of Michigan

sentences.en: Lauren Mullenbach, Assistant Research Scientist in Environmental Justice, University of Michigan
sentences.zh: Lauren Mullenbach，密歇根大学环境正义助理研究科学家

content_html_zh:
数据中心项目继续在全国范围内引发争议。 在某种程度上，这是因为各种不同的群体有着相互竞争的利益——一些人支持他们，一些人反对，另一些人对数据中心本身没有直接看法，但对数据中心运营和影响的各个方面表示担忧。

作为环境正义和城市土地利用的学者，我在密歇根州看到了各种相互冲突的力量在发挥作用。 仅过去两年，该州就提出了 30 多个大大小小的数据中心项目，其中包括我工作的大学的一个。

政府。 格雷琴·惠特默 (Gretchen Whitmer) 热衷于将科技公司引入该州，甚至在拟议的数据中心所在地与科技公司首席执行官合影留念。

但并不是每个人都那么兴奋。 这些项目可能面临的反对只是一个例子，我居住的当地自来水公司伊普西兰蒂社区公用事业管理局告诉州政府，它不会为密歇根大学和洛斯阿拉莫斯国家实验室在其服务区域内提议的数据中心提供冷却水。

因此，密歇根大学提议在邻近的苏必利尔镇建立一个不同的地点。 该镇自己管理水源，但通过从伊普西兰蒂社区公用事业管理局和安娜堡镇购买水来获得供水。

看看围绕这些项目发挥作用的一些力量，就会发现它们提出的深层次问题。 关于数据中心的争论常常表现为公司和社区成员之间的冲突。 但它们也反映了社会价值观、民主制度和资本主义利益的冲突。

科技公司

科技公司显然希望存储和处理更多的数据。 社会的许多关键方面都依赖于几十年来建立的数据中心，为网站提供服务，处理在线购买以及发送电子邮件、短信和警报通知。

最新的推动与商业世界和人们生活中对人工智能系统的预期需求有关，以及由此产生的对计算能力的需求增加，以处理所有这些请求并过滤所有可用信息以提供响应。

企业发展的压力、继续为股东增加利润以及从员工和资产中榨取更多收入的压力，促使这些科技公司寻求更多土地来建设更多数据中心。 密歇根州立法机关公开向科技公司示好，通过了法律，免除数据中心运营商原本必须缴纳的销售税和使用税。

电力公司

许多电力公司受到州或地方政府的监管，不允许通过维持现有服务或销售电力来盈利。 他们的利润来自建造新发电厂、新变压器和变电站以及新电力线，并将这些成本转嫁给客户，并提高利润。

据当地新闻报道，数据中心可能需要在发电和输电方面进行大量投资——例如，谷歌在密歇根州范布伦镇提议的数据中心将需要“2.7吉瓦的电力——相当于大约200万个家庭的电力需求”。

因此，电力公司可能渴望利用新的主要客户的机会，并且可能愿意忍受一定程度的公众强烈反对。

在较小地理区域运营的电力公司高度依赖于它们吸引客户和建设基础设施的城市的成功。 在这些情况下，他们对当地经济增长有既得利益，特别是向提供就业机会和工人的公司迁移到该地区。 这促使他们通过游说分区变更以启用数据中心来参与当地的政治决策。

在区域或整个州运营的大型公用事业公司与其服务区域内特定城市的经济成功联系较少。 但他们也能够将影响力瞄准州立法者。 例如，DTE Energy 向州立法者保证，拟建的范布伦镇数据中心不会提高客户的电费。

社区领袖

社区领袖和民选官员通常对科技公司承诺的数据中心带来的工作感兴趣，因此他们倾向于支持批准许可证、分区修正案或其他法律变更。

尽管数据中心并没有真正创造很多就业机会，但它们确实有可能为当地财产税做出贡献，这可以吸引没有太多其他选择的小镇。

城镇总是在寻找增长机会，寻求额外的税收收入。 这可能会导致他们感受到修建新道路、修改土地使用条例以及批准希望在其境内建设的公司的请求的压力。

我的研究发现，即使在收缩城市或小城市中，这种压力也存在。 随着城市寻求减少居民税收，这种压力有所增加。 他们认为，减少现有居民收入的解决方案是引进更多企业、更多工业和更多新居民。 这使得他们对欢迎数据中心感兴趣。

然而，并非每个社区领袖都支持数据中心。 有些人反对他们，但感到束手无策或无力​​阻止或减缓他们的进展，正如伊普西兰蒂镇和附近的萨林镇所发生的那样。

日常人

在许多社区，关心噪音、土地使用、水和电力使用的普通人反对数据中心。

他们还担心数据中心可能会给公用事业纳税人、纳税人和那些必须承受环境影响的人带来的成本。 通过公开听证会、条例修订和选举等民主程序行使的人民权力可能会被科技公司和结盟团体压垮。

但选民确实有权力。 2026 年 6 月，犹他州选民罢免了长期立法领袖、州参议院主席斯图尔特·亚当斯 (Stuart Adams)，他曾帮助获得在该州西北部建立大型数据中心的批准。

数据中心能否在一个城市向前发展最终取决于政府官员如何理清所有这些参与者的动机和社会政治力量。

Lauren Mullenbach，密歇根大学环境正义助理研究科学家
